reason: Pinyin 'yíbàn' reflects spoken tone sandhi (一 shifts from tone 1 to tone 2 before tone 4). Standard citation-form pinyin would be 'yībàn'. Flagging for a policy decision on whether sandhi tones should be encoded in this dataset.

Two distinct readings with different meanings: diào (to transfer; tone; tune in music) and tiáo (to adjust; to blend; to season food). The entry pins diào but tiáo is equally learner-relevant. One card cannot cover both readings without confusion.
Two distinct readings: lè (happy; joyful) and yuè (music). The entry pins lè but yuè appears in dataset_meanings and cedict. Both readings are common at this HSK level.

Two unrelated primary senses: (1) what one is good at / one's strong point (abstract noun), (2) director or head of an institute/office/station (occupation). The card only covers sense 2; a learner will encounter both at HSK3 level.

Two unrelated primary senses at this level: (1) first day of the lunar month, (2) first year of junior middle school (7th grade). The card picks only sense 1, but sense 2 is equally common in daily Chinese. Both are too important to drop silently.

cedict_en only cross-references 老头子 with no direct definition; dataset_meanings likewise says 'see 老头子'. The authored meanings are attested and correct colloquially, but the source data provides no independent confirmation.
options: старик (разг.) / old man (coll.) — keep as authored
flag for manual verification against a secondary source

reason: Sense split: cedict primary entry is 'to recover/to revert' (returning to normal condition), while the variant 回覆 carries 'to reply'. Dataset lists both. Authored card uses 'to reply (to a letter)' which is the dominant practical use at HSK4, but the primary cedict sense is recovery. Confirm which sense is intended as the card face.
options: to recover; to revert (to a previous state)

reason: cedict lists 'tide' as the first sense (退潮, 涨潮, 潮水 are all common), but the entry picks the adjective sense 'damp; moist'; both senses are primary at HSK4 and the choice affects which tags and RU/EN to use
options: tide; tidal (nature sense) — tags: nature, weather
damp; moist; humid (adjective sense) — tags: manner_state, weather

reason: cedict lists active 'to defeat' first, but the entry gives only the passive sense 'to be defeated; fail'; 打败 in this same batch covers the active sense, so passive focus may be intentional, but the primary cedict gloss is active

'ширпотреб' carries a negative/cheap-goods connotation absent from 百货, which is neutral (general merchandise, as in 百货商店 department store). Options: 'товары широкого потребления', 'товары; универмаг', or simply 'товары (общие)'.

cedict primary senses are 'to move/to transport/to use/to apply' (listed first); 'luck/fortune' are secondary senses. The entry teaches only luck/fortune, which is pedagogically reasonable (运气, 好运) but omits the dominant transport/movement sense used in 运动 and 运输.
options: luck; fortune (current — matches 好运 companion card)
to transport; luck (covers both primary and secondary sense)

reason: Entry authored with pinyin shān covers only the verbal reading (to fan, to slap). The noun reading shàn (fan; classifier for doors and windows) is a distinct, common learner-relevant reading at HSK 5. Consider separate entries or a combined bilingual note.
options: shān: to fan; to slap (verb only, as authored)

reason: Two readings exist: hāi (sigh/interjection — used in this entry) and kě (cough — the reading most learners encounter first). The entry is self-consistent with pinyin hāi, but the more common HSK-5 sense may be kě/cough. Confirm which reading this card is intended to teach.
options: kě — to cough (more frequently seen reading)

reason: Two equally common primary senses at HSK-5: (1) to lose money / deficit (current entry) and (2) luckily / fortunately / thanks to (亏得 usage). Neither is clearly dominant; confirm which sense to foreground.

Pinyin dàdū conflicts with the chosen adverb meaning. The adverb sense (mostly/for the most part) is primarily pronounced dàduō; dàdū is the noun/metropolitan reading (Yuan-dynasty capital). cedict lists dàdū as an alternative for the adverb, but learners need to know which form to say.
options: Correct pinyin to dàduō and keep adverb meaning (mostly; for the most part)
Keep pinyin dàdū and switch meaning to the historical/metropolitan sense, tagging it differently

reason: CEDICT lists two distinct senses: (1) 'by/at the end of the year' (primary, most common) and (2) 'shortly before New Year'. The authored gloss captures only sense 2. Clarify which sense is primary for this card.
options: by the end of the year / at year-end

reason: CEDICT gives two separate senses: (1) 'to see the same thing over and over again' and (2) 'to see, to one's surprise, sth happen suddenly' (narrative particle). The Russian gloss 'и вдруг видишь; видно лишь' conflates them — 'видно лишь' (one sees only) does not match either sense cleanly. Choose one primary sense.
options: literary particle: and then (one sees) / suddenly one sees
to see repeatedly; over and over
